content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, each citizen has their own personal string. Chef is no exception. Recently, his string broke and he went to a shop to get a new one.

There are two strings in the shop: AAA and BBB. Chef may choose any substring AsA_sAsŌĆŗ of AAA (possibly empty) and any substring BsB_sBsŌĆŗ of BBB (also possibly empty). Then, the shop assistant cuts these substrings out and sells a single string C=As+BsC = A_s + B_sC=AsŌĆŗ+BsŌĆŗ (here, +++ denotes string concatenation) to Chef. For example, if AAA is "aba" and BBB is "cde", then some of the strings Chef can buy are "ba", "bac", "bacd", "ae", "d", "acde", "aba", "cde" or "abacde".

Chef has seen a lot of strings in his life and he liked exactly NNN of them (numbered 111 through NNN). For each valid iii, the iii-th of these strings is denoted by SiS_iSiŌĆŗ and according to Chef, it has a beauty bib_ibiŌĆŗ. Let's denote the number of substrings of CCC that are equal to SiS_iSiŌĆŗ by cnti(C)cnt_i(C)cntiŌĆŗ(C). If Chef buys a string CCC, he says that the pleasure he gets from this string is Ōłæi=1Ncnti(C)Ōŗģbi\sum_{i=1}^N cnt_i(C) \cdot b_iŌłæi=1NŌĆŗcntiŌĆŗ(C)ŌŗģbiŌĆŗ.

What is the maximum possible pleasure Chef can get from a string he buys in the shop?
constraints: A,B,S1,S2,ŌĆ”,SNA, B, S_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NA,B,S1ŌĆŗ,S2ŌĆŗ,ŌĆ”,SNŌĆŗ contain only lowercase English letters
S1,S2,ŌĆ”,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1ŌĆŗ,S2ŌĆŗ,ŌĆ”,SNŌĆŗ are pairwise distinct
the sum of ŌłŻAŌłŻ|A|ŌłŻAŌłŻ over all test cases does not exceed 2Ōŗģ1032 \cdot 10^32Ōŗģ103
the sum of ŌłŻBŌłŻ|B|ŌłŻBŌłŻ over all test cases does not exceed 2Ōŗģ1032 \cdot 10^32Ōŗģ103
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2Ōŗģ1042 \cdot 10^42Ōŗģ104
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points, time limit 2 seconds):** ŌłŻAŌłŻ,ŌłŻBŌłŻ,NŌēż100|A|, |B|, N \le 100ŌłŻAŌłŻ,ŌłŻBŌłŻ,NŌēż100
the sum of ŌłŻAŌłŻ|A|ŌłŻAŌłŻ over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
the sum of ŌłŻBŌłŻ|B|ŌłŻBŌłŻ over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single string AAA.
The second line contains a single string BBB.
The third line contains a single integer NNN.
The following NNN lines describe Chef's favourite strings. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a string SiS_iSiŌĆŗ, followed by a space and an integer bib_ibiŌĆŗ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ŌĆĢ the maximum pleasure.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef can buy the string CCC = "tech" + "echef" = "techechef". In this string CCC, one substring is "chef", there are no substrings "code" and two substrings are "eche". Chef's pleasure in this case is 1Ōŗģ3+0Ōŗģ1+2Ōŗģ5=131 \cdot 3 + 0 \cdot 1 + 2 \cdot 5 = 131Ōŗģ3+0Ōŗģ1+2Ōŗģ5=13, which is the maximum possible pleasure.
